title: Conocliniopsis grossedentata
taxonomy: Conocliniopsis grossedentata is a species of plant in the family Aizoaceae. It belongs to the genus Conocliniopsis, which is a group of succulent plants native to South Africa. The Aizoaceae family, also known as the ice plant family, consists of over 140 genera and approximately 2,000 species. This family is part of the larger order Caryophyllales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes succulents and other related species. Conocliniopsis grossedentata's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Conocliniopsis grossedentata, also known as the hairy or gross-toothed bushman's hat, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used in South African folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Conocliniopsis grossedentata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Conocliniopsis grossedentata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve symptoms of fever, inflammation, and skin irritation.
